comments.body: There is a moment. Just before a gift is opened. A moment largely forgotten by our competitors. The first impression you have when you are the privileged recipient of a Feldart gift is this: refined pastel hues, crisp, clean lines, and the weight of it in your hands, which promises quality. luxury. craftsmanship. It is a box marked with our new logo, a matrix of 12 stones. These 12 stones are the 12 gems of the Choshen, the high priest’s breastplate. These 12 Stones, once carried together over the heart, now represent Feldart and our tagline: Gifts from the heart. The people in your life deserve an unboxing experience like no other. Feldart: Judaica gifts chosen with care, presented with intention, and felt long after the wrapping is gone.

Imagine the moment: You have just received a parcel from FeldArt. Your first impression is the weight of it. It feels solid, rigid, expensive. You flick open the lid, the magnets engage, and the lid swings back with a satisfying ‘click’. You are impressed by the soft pastel hues: undulating seas of paint mix with a nebula of muted tones. Your eyes are drawn to a matrix of 12 black dots: our Choshen.

Imagine the moment: You just received a parcel from Feldart. Your first impression is this: This feels heavy. Solid. The edges are crisp and square. You look over the box in your hands. The colours are beautiful. You imagine the pastel swirls as the tails of a distant nebula: Space dust bleeding from a comet in the solar winds of fickle alloys vaporising in the rays of a faraway sun. You follow the widths of the case you are holding and eye 12 black dots. Your right hand feels under the magnetic cover to flip the lid back Your eyes follow your hand and stop at the height of the opened lid, then they fall to the centre of the panel. The 12 black dots on the outside have now been brought close to be revealed as sparkling gems. These are our Choshen: 12 stones, one for each tribe, which were once carried together over the heart of the Kohen Gadol. (Something here to help flow) Gift-giving is about bringing people together, and Feldart takes this to a higher level.

(okay now what level is that? can i squeeze in some chassidus?)
@Samual Kessel It is fantastic, very well written, but it has not got a flow that would captivate the audience. it needs to be very basic language, flow nicely, with a bit of humour and storytelling

Here at Feldart, we have been
working on something new. A total redesign of our packaging. We want to be your
first thought when it comes to luxury Judaica gift-giving.

Imagine you just received a
parcel from Feldart. Your first impression is this: This feels heavy. Solid.
The edges are crisp and square. Soft pastel swirls dance across the box faces.
On the side of your case, you eye 12 black dots. Your right-hand flips back the
magnetic cover. The 12 black dots on the outside are now front and centre,
brought close and revealed as sparkling gems. These are our Choshen. 12 stones,
one for each tribe, which were once carried together over the heart of the
Kohen Gadol. And that’s the embodiment of our philosophy: uniting the tribes through
gift-giving. For ‘Gifts from the heart’, give with Feldart.

the ending is a bit abrupt

I think it needs another couple of lines

I put it through claude and asked to use Shaya's linkedin posts as style reference. Here's the result, tell me if it seems too AI:

I picked it up.

Turned it over in my hands.

Felt the weight. The crisp edges. The soft pastel swirls across the box faces.

Then I noticed the 12 dots on the side.

I flipped back the magnetic cover.

And there they were — up close, revealed. Sparkling gems. Our Choshen. 12 stones, one for each tribe of Israel, once carried over the heart of the Kohen Gadol himself.

That's not packaging. That's a statement.

At Feldart, we've been working on something new. A total redesign. Because when someone receives a gift from us, we want that moment — the lift, the reveal, the this feels different — to begin before they even open the box.

Luxury Judaica gift-giving deserves a first impression worthy of it.

We want to be your first thought when it matters most.

Gifts from the heart. Give with Feldart.
way too AI. Sorry!
